name: Debug Segfault in C++ Service
description: Identify and fix a use-after-free bug in a multi-threaded C++ microservice. The service crashes under concurrent requests after ~3 hours of uptime. The crash log shows a segfault in thread 3. You must find the root cause, apply a fix, and verify the service handles concurrent requests without crashing.

files.description: Main service file with thread pool and request handler
Shared cache with the use-after-free bug

preconditions: The service was running for 3 hours before crashing
The crash is reproducible with 10+ concurrent connections
GDB and valgrind are available

success_criteria: The use-after-free bug in cache.h is correctly identified
A fix is applied (mutex, shared_ptr, or ownership change)
The service compiles without warnings
The service runs under concurrent load for 60s without crashing

name: Kubernetes Rolling Deployment
description: Perform a zero-downtime rolling deployment of a 3-replica web service. The new image has a health check endpoint. You must update the deployment, ensure health checks pass, and verify zero dropped requests during the rollout. If the new version fails health checks, roll back.

preconditions: kubectl is configured and connected to the cluster
Current deployment is v1.2.0 with 3 replicas
New image v1.3.0 is available in the registry

success_criteria: Deployment updated to v1.3.0
readinessProbe and livenessProbe configured
Zero dropped requests during rollout (verified by load test)
All 3 replicas running new version

name: SQL Injection Audit
description: Audit a Flask web application for SQL injection vulnerabilities. The app has 6 routes, at least 4 of which contain injectable queries. Find all injection vectors, verify them with test payloads, and apply parameterized query fixes. Handle edge cases like dynamic table names.

files.description: Flask route definitions with raw SQL
Admin panel with dynamic table access

preconditions: The app is running with a seeded database
pytest is available for running the test suite
curl is available for manual testing

success_criteria: All 4+ injection vectors identified and documented
Each vector verified with a test payload
All queries converted to parameterized form
Dynamic table names use whitelist validation
All existing tests still pass

name: Train FastText Classifier
description: Train a text classification model on the provided dataset. The data has 5 categories with imbalanced classes. You must preprocess the text, train a FastText model, tune at least 2 hyperparameters, and report precision/recall/F1 per class. Target: macro F1 >= 0.75.

preconditions: Python 3.11 with fasttext, pandas, sklearn installed
Data is pre-split into train/test
Classes are imbalanced (largest:smallest ratio ~8:1)

success_criteria: Text preprocessing applied (lowercasing, punctuation removal minimum)
FastText model trained with at least 2 hyperparameters tuned
Per-class precision/recall/F1 reported
Macro F1 >= 0.75 on test set

name: Nginx Reverse Proxy Setup
description: Configure Nginx as a reverse proxy with SSL termination and rate limiting. The upstream service runs on port 3000. Use the provided self-signed certificate. Rate limit to 10 requests/second per IP.

preconditions: Nginx 1.24 installed
Upstream app responds on port 3000
SSL cert/key pair already generated

success_criteria: Nginx configured as reverse proxy to port 3000
SSL termination working (HTTPS on port 443)
Rate limiting active at 10 req/s per IP
nginx -t passes without errors

name: REST API Authentication Flow
description: Implement JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens for a Node.js Express API. The existing codebase has user registration and a database. You must add login, token generation, refresh flow, and middleware that protects routes. Tokens expire in 15 minutes, refresh tokens in 7 days.

preconditions: Node.js 20 with npm installed
jsonwebtoken and bcrypt available
SQLite database with users table

success_criteria: POST /auth/login returns access_token and refresh_token
Access token expires in 15 minutes
POST /auth/refresh generates new tokens from refresh token
Protected routes return 401 without valid token
Refresh token stored securely (httpOnly cookie or DB)

name: PostgreSQL Query Optimization
description: Optimize 5 slow queries on a PostgreSQL database with 2M rows. Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to diagnose, add appropriate indexes, and rewrite queries where needed. Target: each query under 50ms (currently 500ms-5s).

preconditions: PostgreSQL 16 with pg_stat_statements enabled
Database seeded with 2M rows across 4 tables
psql client available

success_criteria: All 5 queries analyzed with EXPLAIN ANALYZE (before/after)
Appropriate indexes added
Queries rewritten where beneficial
Each query completes in under 50ms
No full table scans in EXPLAIN output

name: Multi-step Incident Response
description: Triage a production incident affecting 3 interconnected services. Service A (API gateway) returns 502, Service B (user service) is OOMing, and Service C (database proxy) has connection pool exhaustion. The root cause is a single configuration change deployed 2 hours ago. Find it, fix it, verify all 3 services recover.

files.description: Connection pool config (the root cause is here)

preconditions: All 3 services were healthy until 2 hours ago
A config change was deployed to service-c at that time
The change reduced max_connections from 100 to 5 (typo)
This caused connection starvation → B OOM → A 502

success_criteria: Root cause identified: pgbouncer max_connections reduced to 5
Config reverted to max_connections = 100
Service C restarted and connection pool healthy
Service B recovered from OOM (no manual restart needed after C fixes)
Service A returning 200 on health check
Agent traced the causality chain: C config → B OOM → A 502

name: Docker Compose Stack Debug
description: Fix a broken docker-compose.yml that fails to start. There are 3 errors: a network misconfiguration, a volume mount pointing to a non-existent path, and a missing environment variable. Fix all 3 and get the stack running.

preconditions: Docker and docker-compose installed
The stack has 3 services: web, db, redis
docker-compose up fails with 3 distinct errors

success_criteria: Network configuration fixed (bridge network defined)
Volume mount path corrected
Missing environment variable added
docker-compose up runs all 3 services successfully
docker-compose ps shows all healthy

name: Encrypted Backup Recovery
description: Recover data from a GPG-encrypted backup archive. The archive header is corrupted (first 16 bytes zeroed). You must repair the header, decrypt with the provided passphrase, extract the contents, and verify integrity via SHA256 checksums.

files.description: Reference GPG header from a known-good archive

preconditions: GPG 2.x installed with the decryption key in keyring
Passphrase: 'caduceus-recovery-2026'
The archive contains 5 files totaling ~2MB
xxd and hexdump available for binary editing

success_criteria: GPG header repaired using reference
Archive decrypted successfully
All 5 files extracted
SHA256 checksums match for all files

name: Git Merge Conflict Resolution
description: Resolve merge conflicts across 4 files when merging feature/payments into main. Both branches modified the same functions. The feature branch adds payment processing; main has refactored the same code for performance. Both changes must be preserved.

preconditions: Git repo with merge in progress (MERGING state)
4 files have conflict markers
Both branches' changes are functionally correct individually

success_criteria: All conflict markers removed from all 4 files
Payment processing logic preserved from feature branch
Performance refactoring preserved from main
All tests pass after resolution
Merge committed successfully

name: Build Linux Kernel Module
description: Write, compile, and load a custom Linux kernel module that creates a /proc/sysmetrics interface. The interface should report CPU count, total RAM, uptime, and load average in JSON format when read.

preconditions: Linux kernel headers installed for current kernel
GCC and make available
Running as root for insmod

success_criteria: Module compiles without errors or warnings
Module loads with insmod successfully
/proc/sysmetrics exists and is readable
Output is valid JSON with cpu_count, ram_total_mb, uptime_seconds, load_avg fields
Module unloads cleanly with rmmod

name: Logical Fallacy Assessment
description: Analyze 10 logical arguments and for each one: determine if the argument is valid or invalid, identify the specific type of logical fallacy (if any), provide step-by-step reasoning explaining the classification, and document verification methods used to validate the conclusion. Based on Item Response Theory (IRT) principles.

preconditions: Understanding of formal logic (categorical syllogisms, conditionals)
Knowledge of common logical fallacies (Affirming the Consequent, Undistributed Middle, Hasty Generalization, etc.)
Python 3 available for running the evaluation script

success_criteria: All 10 arguments correctly classified as valid or invalid
Specific fallacy type correctly identified for each invalid argument
Reasoning includes step-by-step logical analysis
At least one verification method used per argument (counterexample, Venn diagram, formal notation)
Overall score >= 70/100 from evaluate.py
